title: Strata — an optimizing compiler to WebAssembly
description: A from-scratch optimizing compiler that turns a small C-like language into real WebAssembly bytecode in your browser: SSA, SCCP/GVN/LICM/DCE/inlining, if-conversion to branchless select, and a relooper backend. Does floating point from scratch, both directions: str(float) is a genuine Dragon4 shortest-round-trip formatter written in the language itself, and parse_float its correctly-rounded inverse. Now ships a full transcendental math library (exp/ln/log2/log10/sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/atan2/sinh/cosh/tanh/pow/cbrt/hypot/fmod, plus sqrt/floor/round/…) written ONCE as a shared Strata kernel that the wasm backend compiles and the reference interpreter runs — so they agree bit-for-bit, and a dedicated oracle proves each is within ~1 ULP of libm. Plus a real f32 single-precision type lowered end to end to the wasm f32 opcodes (arithmetic, compare, conversions, f32[], f32 struct fields), structs with nested/recursive layouts and a null handle, 64-bit long → wasm i64 (BigInt-exact, hex/L literals, long[], str(long)), bit primitives (popcount/clz/ctz/rotl/rotr), do-while/switch, a string standard library with str[] (split/join), a step debugger, and a differential test suite proving the wasm matches a reference interpreter at every opt level. NEW: first-class functions — a fn(…)->R type whose values are real function pointers, called through a wasm function table and call_indirect, usable in variables/params/returns/struct-field vtables, with a devirtualization pass that turns provable indirect calls back into direct ones at -O1+; this unlocks genuine higher-order programming (map/reduce, a comparator sort, compose). NEWEST: arrays of function pointers — (fn(…)->…)[] via fn_array(n) — a real jump table / state machine, with function-table slot 0 reserved as a null funcref so an unset slot traps on call and fn == null is an observable 'is this wired up?' test; the flagship is a stack-based bytecode VM whose entire decode step is one table[op](vm, imm) lowering to call_indirect. All proven by the differential harness (716 checks across -O0..-O3). NEWEST: division/remainder by a constant — strength reduction that turns every `/ C` and `% C` (32- AND 64-bit) into the textbook multiply/shift/add sequences: power-of-two shifts with a round-toward-zero bias, and the signed magic-number multiply of Hacker's Delight, including a from-scratch 64-bit high-multiply synthesized purely from i64 ops since wasm has no mulhi. At -O1+ the hardware i32.div_s/i64.div_s opcodes vanish entirely, each rewrite an EXACT identity (never an approximation) proven bit-for-bit against the reference interpreter at every level and fuzzed over millions of dividends including INT_MIN/INT_MAX. NEWEST: MEMORY OPTIMIZATION — the first pass to reason about linear memory. An alias analysis (address → base SSA value + constant byte offset; same-base disjoint ranges proven non-aliasing, any different base conservatively may-alias) drives a forward available-memory dataflow (a MUST analysis, meet = intersection, iterated to a fixpoint) that does store→load forwarding (a field written then read back becomes the value), redundant-load elimination (the same element loaded twice loads once), dead-store elimination (a field overwritten before any read isn't stored) and silent-store elimination (writing a value a cell already holds is a no-op). Conservative by precondition — a call clears all facts, print is a read-only barrier, i8 byte stores are never forwarded — so it can only ever miss, never miscompile; a particle step()'s read-modify-write burst collapses from 8 memory ops to 1 at -O1, proven behaviour-preserving by the differential oracle. NEWEST: SROA — ESCAPE ANALYSIS + SCALAR REPLACEMENT OF AGGREGATES, the optimization that makes the record DISAPPEAR. Struct construction lowers to a first-class `alloc` IR op (the string runtime keeps the raw bump sequence, so a fresh allocation is never confused with a heap-pointer rewind — the exact soundness subtlety that blocked this before). Escape analysis traces each handle and every address derived from it by adding a constant: if it is only ever the base of its own field loads/stores — never stored, returned, compared, passed to a call, or merged through a phi — the record is provably private and aliases nothing, so each field is promoted out of memory by FULL CYTRON SSA CONSTRUCTION (dominance-frontier phi placement over the field's store sites + a dominator-tree rename), turning a field written before a branch into a phi at the merge and one accumulated in a loop into a loop-header phi. Because the handle provably aliases nothing, promotion forwards straight across calls — where the memory pass must stay conservative — and memopt now proves two distinct allocations disjoint. The 2D-vectors example at -O2 (helpers inlined) drops from 164 wasm instructions / 506 bytes to 12 / 139 — all four allocations and 21 memory ops gone — and a Particle step() melts to ZERO allocs and ZERO memory ops at -O1, conditional bounce writes and all. Every promotion an exact rewrite, proven bit-for-bit by the differential oracle. NEWEST: 128-BIT SIMD VECTORS — first-class data parallelism lowered to real wasm v128. Four shapes (int4=i32x4, float4=f32x4, long2=i64x2, double2=f64x2) are full value types: locals, parameters, return values, loop-carried phis, even a v128 typed-select out of if-conversion. Elementwise operators map one-to-one to the lane ops (f32x4.add/sub/mul/div, i32x4 add/sub/mul, whole-vector v128.and/or/xor/not for &|^~), with a builtin library for everything else: int4(a,b,c,d) / int4(x) splat, lane(v,k) / withlane(v,k,x) (constant lane immediates), hsum reduction, vmin/vmax/vsqrt/vabs, the six lanewise comparisons veq/vne/vlt/vle/vgt/vge that yield an integer mask, vselect(mask,a,b) (a bit-exact v128.bitselect), and to_float4/to_int4 (saturating f32x4↔i32x4 convert). The whole family flows untouched through the optimizer — GVN deduplicates lane ops, DCE drops dead vectors, the stackifier folds single-use vectors straight onto the operand stack — and the reference interpreter models each lane with the matching rounding (Math.fround per f32 lane, i32/i64 wrap, byte-exact bitselect), so the headline branchless SIMD-Mandelbrot example (four pixels per step, escape counts accumulated through a compare mask) is proven identical to the wasm at every level. All told the differential oracle now runs 836 checks across -O0..-O3.
